papers.title: Detecting Safety Violations Across Many Agent Traces
papers.reason: The abstract directly addresses the critical intersection of safety auditing and evaluation for agentic systems by proposing a novel method to detect complex, multi-trace violations that existing benchmarks miss.

papers.title: A Mechanistic Analysis of Looped Reasoning Language Models
papers.reason: The abstract provides a deep mechanistic analysis of a novel LLM architecture (looped reasoning) and its inference dynamics, directly addressing architectural design and optimization strategies.

papers.title: ClawGuard: A Runtime Security Framework for Tool-Augmented LLM Agents Against Indirect Prompt Injection
papers.reason: The abstract directly addresses a critical safety vulnerability (indirect prompt injection) specific to agentic systems and proposes a novel runtime framework that enforces security at the tool-call boundary without altering the underlying LLM architecture.

papers.title: ClawGUI: A Unified Framework for Training, Evaluating, and Deploying GUI Agents
papers.reason: The abstract details a comprehensive framework for GUI agents covering RL training, standardized evaluation, persistent memory, and deployment across devices, directly addressing core topics in agentic systems and training methods.

papers.title: Retrieval Is Not Enough: Why Organizational AI Needs Epistemic Infrastructure
papers.reason: The abstract directly addresses RAG limitations by proposing a novel epistemic memory structure and a new evaluation methodology for agentic systems, though it focuses on knowledge representation rather than core LLM architecture or inference optimization.

papers.title: Agentic Aggregation for Parallel Scaling of Long-Horizon Agentic Tasks
papers.reason: The abstract directly addresses parallel test-time scaling for agentic tasks, proposing a novel aggregation mechanism that optimizes inference efficiency and coordinates multiple trajectories, fitting the core themes of agentic systems and inference optimization.

papers.title: SWE-AGILE: A Software Agent Framework for Efficiently Managing Dynamic Reasoning Context
papers.reason: The abstract directly addresses the core challenges of agentic systems in software engineering by proposing a novel memory management strategy (sliding window and reasoning digests) to optimize inference efficiency and context handling.

papers.title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
papers.reason: Landmark paper on memory-efficient LLM finetuning — highly relevant.

papers.title: Evaluating Cooperation in LLM Social Groups through Elected Leadership
papers.reason: The abstract directly addresses multi-agent coordination and agentic systems by proposing and evaluating leadership mechanisms to improve social welfare in LLM simulations.

papers.title: Legal2LogicICL: Improving Generalization in Transforming Legal Cases to Logical Formulas via Diverse Few-Shot Learning
papers.reason: The paper directly addresses RAG for few-shot learning, proposes a novel LLM architecture for legal reasoning, introduces a new evaluation dataset, and discusses training-free methods, though it lacks focus on agentic systems, memory, or safety.

papers.title: Solving Physics Olympiad via Reinforcement Learning on Physics Simulators
papers.reason: The paper focuses on a novel training methodology using physics simulators to enhance LLM reasoning, which directly addresses training methods and implicitly influences architecture capabilities.

papers.title: CLSGen: A Dual-Head Fine-Tuning Framework for Joint Probabilistic Classification and Verbalized Explanation
papers.reason: The abstract proposes a novel fine-tuning framework and architecture for LLMs to improve probability estimation and evaluation metrics while preserving explanation capabilities.

papers.title: LangFlow: Continuous Diffusion Rivals Discrete in Language Modeling
papers.reason: The paper introduces a novel continuous diffusion architecture for language modeling with specific training innovations, directly addressing LLM architecture and training methods while offering implications for inference optimization.

papers.title: Collaborative Multi-Agent Scripts Generation for Enhancing Imperfect-Information Reasoning in Murder Mystery Games
papers.reason: The paper directly addresses multi-agent coordination, training methods (GRPO, fine-tuning), and evaluation of VLMs in complex reasoning scenarios, though it focuses less on RAG, memory, or safety.

papers.title: DreamKG: A KG-Augmented Conversational System for People Experiencing Homelessness
papers.reason: The abstract describes a hybrid RAG system integrating knowledge graphs with LLMs for reliable, context-aware inference, directly addressing architecture and optimization for specific query types.

papers.title: Playing Along: Learning a Double-Agent Defender for Belief Steering via Theory of Mind
papers.reason: The paper introduces a novel safety-focused evaluation benchmark for theory-of-mind and adversarial deception, utilizing reinforcement learning to train models, but does not address architecture, agentic systems, RAG, memory, inference optimization, or multi-agent coordination.
